title: Israel claims to have killed Iran's security chief Ali Larijani | ABC NEWS
description: Israeli authorities claim to have assassinated Iran's security chief Ali Larijani as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed to continue attacks on Tehran’s senior officials.  Ali Larijani was described as the country’s de facto leader and reportedly commanded the respect of Islamist organisations such as the Iranian Revolutionary Guard more than President Masoud Pezeshkian. title: Kyle Sandilands sacked by ARN Media | ABC NEWS
description: Sydney shock jock Kyle Sandilands has had his contract with KIIS FM terminated. Sandilands had been suspended after an on-air row with co-host Jackie "O" Henderson on February 13.  In a statement, the veteran broadcaster accused the KIIS FM owner ARN Media of using the incident as an excuse to get out of their $100 million deal with him. title: Jim Chalmers responds to interest rate hike and the cost of living squeeze | ABC NEWS
description: Jim Chalmers speaks to News Breakfast the morning after the Reserve Bank of Australia hiked interest rates to 4.1 percent. He says he understands Australians are under significant cost of living pressure.  Chalmers also spoke about his government's plans for Capital Gains Tax reform saying Labor is 'considering options' and it will be Cabinet making the final decision, not a Senate committee. title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle could make three landfalls in Qld, NT and WA | ABC NEWS
description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is barrelling towards Cape York in Far North Queensland as the system is expected to intensify into the strongest storm since Tropical Cyclone Trevor in 2019.  The Bureau of Meteorology is warning of a possibility that the system could make three separate landfalls across Queensland, the Northern Territory and Western Australia.  Flood watch alerts have been issued for regions between the Lockhart River down to Cairns and Ingham. title: Death of Ali Larijani may complicate US efforts to end the war with Iran: expert | ABC NEWS
description: Israel says it has killed top Iranian official and security chief Ali Larijani. Larijani was incredibly influential within Iran’s top brass, with the apparent backing of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). Barbara Slavin from foreign affairs think tank Stimson Center interviewed Larijani and says he was a key figure in diplomatic relations with the United States and was central to Iran’s nuclear negotiations in the early 2000s. Slavin also says his death may complicate US efforts to end the war with Iran.
